page: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 11,339 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% on stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to top-tier scores in extended instruction and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier throughput.

This server is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require high processing density without the overhead of massive memory allocations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient choice for CPU-bound tasks such as high-throughput web serving, database operations, and cryptographic processing. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM prompt processing, though text generation speeds are more moderate. The substantial 11,339 GB local storage capacity supports large-scale data processing, CI/CD pipelines, and high-performance database hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource-dense 2.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 11,339 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The inclusion of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets enhances its cryptographic and vector processing capabilities. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads, making it well-suited for high-throughput web serving, large-scale database operations, and parallelized data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor, 384 GB of RAM, and 11,339 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 features 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11,339 GB local storage, optimized for high-performance compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive local storage, and advanced instruction sets for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory and 11,339 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and vector math
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, databases, and parallel computing
